title: Advanced RAG Patterns Capstone
concept: Designing a routed, agentic, self-correcting RAG system for a multi-source enterprise use case; 'Next up: MCP Foundations.'.

exercises.prompt: In our last lesson, we looked at merging results across multiple sources. What is a key step when combining search outputs. What is a key step when combining search pipelines. What are the key steps when combining search results from distinct hits?

options.text: Converting all structured SQL data into unstructured vector embeddings.
Removing duplicate information and ranking the combined results by relevance.
Deleting older data sources to prevent the model from reading them.

exercises.explain: When merging results from multiple search indexes, duplicate entries must be removed (deduplication) and the remaining chunks must be ranked by relevance so the LLM gets the best context.

cards.title: Putting It All Together
cards.body: We know how to route queries and merge search results from different databases. But what happens if the retrieved documents are completely irrelevant, or if one source contradicts another? We need a smart system that can double-check its own homework!

cards.title: Self-Correcting RAG
cards.body: A self-correcting, agentic RAG system combines dynamic query routing with a feedback loop. It checks if the retrieved documents are relevant, and if not, it automatically rewrites the search query or tries a different data source before generating the final answer.

cards.title: The Smart Research Assistant
cards.body: Imagine an expert research assistant. Instead of just grabbing the first book they see and summarizing it blindly, they check if the book actually answered your question. If they find the source was missing details, they walk back to the library shelves, grab a different book, and try again until they have a complete, verified answer.

cards.title: Enterprise Rescue Loop
cards.body: A customer asks for their invoice status. The router queries the Invoice DB but finds nothing. Instead of giving up, the self-correcting agent detects the empty results, rewrites the query to search the CRM logs, finds the payment, and successfully answers the user.

cards.title: RAG is Not a One-Way Street
cards.body: Many believe RAG is always a straight, one-way street from query to answer. In reality, advanced production RAG is cyclical: it loops, evaluates, and retries search steps dynamically until it meets a specific quality threshold.

cards.title: Linear vs. Agentic Loop
cards.body: Contrast a simple, direct RAG pipeline with an agentic, self-correcting loop.

left.text: Query ➔ Retrieve ➔ Generate. If the search results are bad, it generates a bad answer anyway.

right.text: Query ➔ Retrieve ➔ Grade. If bad, rewrite and loop back. If good ➔ Generate.

exercises.prompt: What is the main role of a Query Router in a multi-source RAG system?

options.text: It translates all database entries into English.
It analyzes the query and directs it to the specific database best suited to answer it.
It compresses the vector database to save memory.
It generates synthetic user questions to train the LLM.

exercises.explain: Query routing acts as the initial dispatcher, sending the incoming question to the most appropriate database (e.g., API vs. Vector Database) based on what the user is asking.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A self-correcting RAG system can decide to rewrite a search query if the initial search results are graded as low quality.
exercises.explain: An agentic RAG system can run a checker step to evaluate if the retrieved chunks are relevant, and if they are not, it can trigger a new search with a modified query.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing term to describe the corrective mechanism in agentic RAG:
exercises.sentence: To fix poor search results, the agent utilizes a feedback loop to evaluate context quality and retry.
exercises.explain: A feedback loop allows the agent to evaluate the quality of the retrieved chunks and decide whether to search again or proceed to generation.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following describes an AI agent performing self-correcting RAG?

options.text: A system that searches a vector database, merges the top 3 hits, and sends them directly to the LLM generator.
A database script that automatically backtracks and deletes old records when they expire.
An agent that searches a database, grades the results as 'low relevance', rewrites the query, and searches a secondary SQL database instead.

exercises.explain: Option C represents an agentic RAG loop where the system grades the retrieved context and decides to rewrite the query and search a different database when the first attempt fails. Options A and B are simple linear retrievals without evaluation or corrective loops.

exercises.prompt: You are designing a self-correcting enterprise RAG agent. Which architecture best describes a fully agentic, self-correcting loop for multi-source retrieval?

options.text: A basic pipeline that queries a single vector database and returns whatever chunk is closest in distance.
A system that routes queries dynamically, evaluates retrieved context quality, and loops back to fetch new data or rewrite queries if the quality is low.
A static pipeline that forces all data sources to merge into a single massive index before searching.
An agent that bypasses retrieval entirely and relies solely on the LLM's internal pre-trained knowledge base.

exercises.explain: An agentic, self-correcting RAG system uses a routing layer to query the right databases, evaluates the retrieved context quality using a grader, and loops back to rewrite the query or fetch new sources if the retrieved data is insufficient. This ensures high-quality answers without human intervention. Next up: MCP Foundations.
